The block-on-plane force cases are limiting/no-motion equilibrium conditions.
The rough-plane follow-up explicitly uses work done against friction and an energy method.

The post-driving impact keeps the object and post together, so the collision/impact stage controls the question.
A three-particle pulley/table system moves from limiting equilibrium to accelerated motion with tensions.
The smooth-plane connected-particle follow-up explicitly requests an energy method.
A particle held at rest by two strings is a static equilibrium resolution problem.
A ring kept at rest on a smooth circular wire by a string is a static force-balance problem.
Two particles meeting after acceleration functions is a straight-line kinematics collision-time task, not momentum.
Connected particles on rough inclined planes are held in limiting equilibrium.
The block speed after work done against resistance is controlled by work-energy.
A vertical collision between two particles with given masses controls the middle of the question.
Hammerhead and nail remain in contact after impact, so the impact/momentum stage controls the question.

Connected particles over a pulley move from equilibrium into released rough-plane motion.
